pi_items.metadata_value: Figure 2: Typical Sharkey clay soil profile (0–150 cm) showing vertic properties and slickensides in the Bt horizon.
pi_items.verified_value: Figure 2: Typical Sharkey clay soil profile (0–150 cm) showing vertic properties and slickensides in the Bssg horizon (NOT Bt horizon).
pi_items.source: USDA Official Series Description for SHARKEY: https://soilseries.sc.egov.usda.gov/OSD_Docs/S/SHARKEY.html states 'No Bt Horizon Present' and shows Bssg horizons from 10-84+ inches. Sharkey is classified as Epiaquert (Vertisol) with slickenside horizons, not argillic horizons.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetic data: images are procedurally generated using ImageMagick, and the Word document is created using python-docx. However, the domain content is grounded in real soil science. Tallahatchie County, Mississippi is a real location with a documented 1970 soil survey. Sharkey clay is a real, extensively-mapped soil series in the USDA soil classification system exhibiting vertic properties and slickensides. The task requires inserting three synthetic images and adding captions. NOTE: The caption for Figure 2 contains a factual soil science error—it references a 'Bt horizon' when the official USDA description of Sharkey soils explicitly states 'No Bt Horizon Present.' Sharkey soils are Vertisols (Epiaquerts) characterized by Bssg (slickenside-gleyed) horizons, not argillic Bt horizons. This is a contradictory claim to authoritative USDA soil taxonomy.
